ex_src: The children had to run to keep up with their father.
ex_tgt: 孩子们得一路跑着才能跟上他们的父亲。

ex_src: I can run a mile in five minutes.
ex_tgt: 我可以在5分钟里跑1英里。

ex_src: The sheep ran away/off in fright.
ex_tgt: 羊受惊跑掉了。

ex_src: A little girl ran up to (= came quickly beside) me, crying for her daddy.
ex_tgt: 一个小女孩跑到我身边，哭着让我替她找爸爸。

ex_src: In the semi-final she will be running against her nearest rival.
ex_tgt: 半决赛中，她将与水平最接近自己的对手一决高下。

ex_src: The first two races will be run (= will happen) in 20 minutes.
ex_tgt: 头两个赛跑项目将在20分钟后举行。

ex_src: Thompson Stables are running three horses in the next race.
ex_tgt: 汤普森赛马训练公司将派出3匹马参加下一场比赛。

ex_src: Would you run to the post office and get me some stamps?
ex_tgt: 你能不能赶快到邮局给我买一些邮票来？

ex_src: You don't put on weight when you spend all day running around after small children.
ex_tgt: 你整天不歇脚地跟在小孩们后面转是胖不起来的。

ex_src: I ran for the bus but it drove off.
ex_tgt: 我跑着去赶公共汽车，但是车却开走了。

ex_src: I run on the spot to warm up before I start training.
ex_tgt: 我在开始训练前原地踏步跑做准备活动。

ex_src: Trains are still running, despite the snow.
ex_tgt: 尽管下了雪，列车仍在运行。

ex_src: A bus runs (= goes on a particular route at particular times) three times a day into town.
ex_tgt: 有一趟公共汽车每天3次开往城里。

ex_src: Skis are waxed on the bottom so that they run smoothly over the snow.
ex_tgt: 滑雪板底部打了蜡，这样就能在雪上滑得顺畅自如。

ex_src: The route/railway/road runs (= goes) across the border/into Italy/through the mountains.
ex_tgt: 这条路线／铁道／公路穿过国界／通向意大利境内／在群山中穿行。

ex_src: A climbing rose bush runs (= grows) up the side of the door.
ex_tgt: 一丛攀缘蔷薇盘绕在门侧。

ex_src: There's a beautiful cornice running around/round all the ceilings.
ex_tgt: 所有的天花板四边都装饰着漂亮的檐口。

ex_src: The film runs (= lasts) for two hours.
ex_tgt: 这部电影长两个小时。

ex_src: The show/course/film runs (= continues) for another week.
ex_tgt: 演出／课程／电影播放将再继续一周。

ex_src: A magazine subscription usually only runs (= can be used) for one year.
ex_tgt: 杂志订阅期限通常只能是一年。

ex_src: Buses are running an hour late, because of an earlier accident.
ex_tgt: 因为先前的一起车祸，公共汽车的运行晚点一小时。

ex_src: The truck's brakes failed and it ran (= went) off the road.
ex_tgt: 卡车刹车失灵，冲出了马路。

ex_src: Trains run on rails (= move along on top of them).
ex_tgt: 火车在铁轨上行驶。

ex_src: Electricity is running through (= moving along within) this cable.
ex_tgt: 电流正通过这条电缆。

ex_src: An angry muttering ran through (= went through) the crowd.
ex_tgt: 人群中传出一阵低沉而愤怒的抱怨声。

ex_src: A shiver of fear ran through his (body).
ex_tgt: 一阵恐惧的战栗传遍他全身。

ex_src: She ran her finger along/down the page/list, looking for her name.
ex_tgt: 她用手指一行行划过那张纸／名单寻找自己的名字。

ex_src: Could you run the tape/film/video back/forwards, please?
ex_tgt: 你可以把磁带／电影／录像带倒回去／向前快进一下吗？

ex_src: Could you possibly run me (= take me in your car) home/to the station?
ex_tgt: 你能否开车把我送回家／到车站？

ex_src: He ran (= pushed) his fingers through his hair and looked up at me.
ex_tgt: 他用手梳拢了一下头发，抬头看我。

ex_src: Keep clear of the machines while they're running.
ex_tgt: 机器运转时不要靠近。

ex_src: The government took desperate measures to keep the economy running.
ex_tgt: 政府采取孤注一掷的措施来维持经济的运行。

ex_src: Do you know how to run this sort of machinery?
ex_tgt: 你会操作这种机器吗？

ex_src: The mechanic asked me to run the engine (= switch it on and allow it to work) for a minute.
ex_tgt: 修车工人让我发动引擎转一分钟。

ex_src: They had the new computer system up and running (= working) within an hour.
ex_tgt: 他们一小时之内就使新的计算机系统运行起来了。

ex_src: We've run the computer program, but nothing happens.
ex_tgt: 我们已经运行了这个计算机程序，但是没有起作用。

ex_src: We're running (= doing) an experiment.
ex_tgt: 我们正在做一个试验。

ex_src: The football team asked the coach which play they should run next.
He's been running a restaurant/his own company since he left school.
ex_tgt: 他毕业后就一直在经营自己的饭店／公司。

ex_src: The local college runs (= provides) a course in self-defence.
ex_tgt: 当地的一所大学开了一门自我防卫课。

ex_src: a well-run/badly-run organization/business/course
ex_tgt: 管理良好／不善的组织；经营良好／很差的公司；设置得很好／差的课程

ex_src: Ruth runs a tight ship and has no time for shirkers.
ex_tgt: 露丝执行了有效的严格管理，员工没有办法开小差。

ex_src: I can't afford to run a car.
ex_tgt: 我养不起车。

ex_src: Some people run their lives according to the movements of the stars.
ex_tgt: 一些人按照星体的运行规律来安排自己的生活。

ex_src: I can feel trickles of sweat running down my neck.
ex_tgt: 我可以感觉到津津汗水顺着脖子往下流淌。

ex_src: Don't cry, or your make-up will run (= become liquid and move down your face).
ex_tgt: 别哭了，要不然化的妆就要花了。

ex_src: The walls were running with damp.
ex_tgt: 墙受潮出现了斑斑渍迹。

ex_src: The river runs (down) to/into the sea.
ex_tgt: 这条河流入大海。

ex_src: The hot tap is running cold (= producing cold water)!
ex_tgt: 热水龙头竟然流出了冷水！

ex_src: I turned the tap on and ran some cold water on the burn.
ex_tgt: 我打开水龙头用凉水冲被烫伤的地方。

ex_src: I'll run you a hot bath (= fill a bath with water for you).
ex_tgt: 我去给你放洗澡水。

ex_src: My nose and eyes have been running all week because of hay fever.
ex_tgt: 我得了干草热，一星期都在流鼻涕淌眼泪。

ex_src: After twelve hours at her computer, the words began to run into one another (= seem mixed together).
ex_tgt: 她在文字处理机前工作了12小时后，看屏幕上的字都觉得模糊一片了。

ex_src: I must have washed my dress at too high a temperature, because the colour has run.
ex_tgt: 我洗这件裙子时用的水肯定太热了，因为都褪色了。

ex_src: If the first layer isn't dry before you add the next one, the colours will run into each other (= mix).
ex_tgt: 如果第一层没有干就涂上第二层，那两层的颜色就会渗在一起。

ex_src: Differences between the two sides run deep (= are serious).
ex_tgt: 双方的分歧加剧了。

ex_src: The river/reservoir/well ran dry (= its supply of water finished).
ex_tgt: 河水／水库／水井干涸了。

ex_src: Supplies are running low (= there's not much left).
ex_tgt: 储备所剩无几了。

ex_src: We're beginning to run short of money/Money is beginning to run short (= there's not much left).
ex_tgt: 我们的钱快花完了／钱快花完了。

ex_src: Oh no, my tights have run!
ex_tgt: 哎呀，糟了，我的连裤袜抽丝了！

ex_src: All the newspapers ran (= printed) stories about the new peace talks.
ex_tgt: 所有报纸都刊登了新一轮和谈的消息。

ex_src: Channel 4 is running a series on the unfairness of the legal system.
ex_tgt: 第四频道正在播出揭露法律体系不公正之处的系列节目。

ex_src: What's running at the the Metro this week?
ex_tgt: 这周Metro电影院在放什么电影？

ex_src: Mrs Thatcher wanted to run a fourth time.
ex_tgt: 撒切尔夫人想要第四次参加竞选。

ex_src: He's going to run against Smith/for president/for re-election.
ex_tgt: 他将要和史密斯作为对手进行竞选／竞选总统／竞选连任。

ex_src: She is considering running for office.
ex_tgt: 她正在考虑参加竞选。

ex_src: He was arrested for running drugs across the border into America.
ex_tgt: 他因为越境向美国走私毒品而被捕。

ex_src: We go for/do a three-mile run every evening after work.
ex_tgt: 我们每天傍晚下班后都会跑上3英里。

ex_src: If you set off at a run (= running), you'll be exhausted later.
ex_tgt: 如果你一开始就跑，过些时候你就会筋疲力尽的。

ex_src: The number of aircraft on the New York-Moscow run is being increased.
ex_tgt: 纽约—莫斯科之间的航线正在增加航班。

ex_src: Let's go for a run (out) in the car somewhere.
ex_tgt: 让我们开车到（外面）什么地方兜兜风吧。

ex_src: The plane swooped in on its bombing run.
ex_tgt: 飞机猛然俯冲下来轰炸。

ex_src: The musical's London run was a disaster.
ex_tgt: 该音乐剧在伦敦的演出彻底失败。

ex_src: They're doing a run at the Cambridge Playhouse.
ex_tgt: 他们正在多玛仓库剧院进行连续演出。

ex_src: There's been a run on umbrellas because of all this rain.
ex_tgt: 就因为这场雨，大家都在抢购雨伞。

ex_src: A sudden run on the dollar has lowered its value.
ex_tgt: 突然出现的抛售导致美元贬值。

ex_src: a run of successes/defeats/bad luck
ex_tgt: 一个又一个的成功／一连串的失败／厄运连连

ex_src: Their food is the general run of hotel cooking.
ex_tgt: 他们的饭菜是一般宾馆饭菜的水平。

ex_src: a sheep/chicken/hen run
ex_tgt: 牧羊场／养鸡场

ex_src: England need 105 runs to win the game.
ex_tgt: 英格兰队需要得到105分才能赢得比赛。

ex_src: The pitcher allowed three runs in just two innings.
I've got a run in my tights from the nail on my chair.
ex_tgt: 我的连裤袜被椅子上的钉子刮了一下，抽丝了。
